Real-data disturbance–response IV — wavelet synchrony (coherence, cross-power, phase → apparent lag, amplitude)

The Morlet-wavelet approach on real MacroSheds precip→discharge — how the native signal-processing view holds up off the idealised bench (cf. analysis II), read against analysis III’s peaks

nic (nic@cascadeclimate.org)

2026-07-17

This is analysis II’s method — Morlet wavelet coherence via WaveletComp — run on real watersheds, the wavelet counterpart to analysis III’s peak/moments pass on the same five sites. The study is a methods comparison: analysis II (syn_data/analysis_02) established, on a clean synthetic system, what the native wavelet view sees that peaks cannot (coupling resolved jointly in time and frequency; a sharp sub-step lag) — and its characteristic failure: the normalized, smoothed coherence can be fooled, staying ≈1 even over noise, so the honest signal lives in the un-normalized cross-power and band amplitude. Here we test whether those strengths and that weakness reproduce on less-deterministic real synchrony: climate = daily precipitation (undisturbed driver), log(response) = log daily discharge, read at the annual P≈365 d band.

Four band-localized quantities, all read at P≈365 (the seasonal band):

  • Wavelet coherence ∈[0,1] — normalized (smoothed) coupling strength.
  • Cross-wavelet power — strength of shared oscillation at the band (un-normalized).
  • Phase difference φ (radians) — Angle = phase.climate − phase.response; climate leads ⇒ positive φ. Shown alongside its step-unit form:
  • Apparent lag = φ · P / (2π) days — φ rescaled to “how many days behind.”
  • Amplitude ratio — band amplitude response / climate (rescaled back to physical units; see the ×sd note in band_trace).

Two caveats carried throughout. (1) COI/edge: at period P the Morlet spans ~±516 days, so band metrics are only trustworthy ≥516 d from a series end and from the disturbance — settled averages use those buffered windows, trace edges are blanked. (2) Uncertainty: the band series is heavily autocorrelated, so error bars use a block SE — one estimate per P-long (annual) cycle, SE = dispersion/√n_blocks (a circular dispersion for phase). Unlike the synthetic windows (~2 cycles), the real windows hold ~15–28 years, so these SEs are far better resolved here.

Provenance & framing. Discharge is fully observed; precip is partly modelled infill (negligible at hbef/fernow, ~13% at hjandrews, ~88% at MC06 — a modelled driver). And a long-term trend sits above the analyzed band (upper period ≈1032 d), so loess.span = 0 (no detrending) is retained without trend-contaminating the seasonal read. Real precip is episodic, not a clean sine, so — as analysis III already hinted — expect coherence to saturate and the power channels to carry whatever real story there is.

Helpers

# ---- small numerics -------------------------------------------------------------------------
smooth_ma <- function(x, w = 61) as.numeric(stats::filter(x, rep(1 / w, w)))  # centered MA; NA at ±(w-1)/2 ends
CMEAN     <- function(a) Arg(mean(exp(1i * a)))          # circular mean of angles (phase never averaged linearly)
band_rows <- function(wc) which(abs(wc$Period - P) <= 0.05 * P)  # the few scale-rows within ±5% of P

# wc.image styling is identical across all five site spectra — only the object + title vary.
plot_wc <- function(wc, main) {
  op <- par(bg = "white", fg = "black", col.axis = "black", col.lab = "black", col.main = "black")
  on.exit(par(op))
  wc.image(wc, which.image = "wc", plot.arrow = TRUE, plot.contour = FALSE,
           color.key = "interval", timelab = "time (days)", periodlab = "period (days)",
           legend.params = list(lab = "wavelet coherence"), main = main)
}

# ---- WaveletComp wrapper --------------------------------------------------------------------
# Analyzed on climate (precip) vs log(response) (log discharge).
# loess.span = 0: no detrending — the analyzed band (≈129–1032 d) sits below any decadal trend,
#   and standardization is then exactly /sd(x), making the ×sd amplitude rescale in band_trace exact.
# my.pair ⇒ Angle = phase.climate − phase.log_response, so climate-leads is +.
# lower/upperPeriod bracket the annual P by ±1.5 octaves so the seasonal ridge sits mid-axis.
wco <- function(df)
  analyze.coherency(as.data.frame(df[, c("climate", "log_response")]),
                    my.pair = c("climate", "log_response"), loess.span = 0,
                    dt = 1, dj = 1/20, lowerPeriod = P / 2^1.5, upperPeriod = P * 2^1.5,
                    make.pval = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)

# ---- collapse the wc object to one time-resolved series at the P band ------------------------
band_trace <- function(wc, df) {
  bi   <- band_rows(wc)
  sd_c <- sd(df$climate); sd_r <- sd(df$log_response)      # the constants WaveletComp divided out (loess.span=0)
  ang  <- apply(wc$Angle[bi, , drop = FALSE], 2, CMEAN)    # per-time band phase difference
  tibble(time      = df$time,
         phase     = df$phase,
         coherence = colMeans(wc$Coherence[bi, , drop = FALSE]),
         xpower    = colMeans(wc$Power.xy[bi, , drop = FALSE]),                 # shared-oscillation power at band
         amp_ratio = colMeans(wc$Ampl.y[bi, , drop = FALSE]) /
                     colMeans(wc$Ampl.x[bi, , drop = FALSE]) * (sd_r / sd_c),   # ×sd: undo standardization → physical band-amp ratio
         angle     = ang,
         lag       = ang / (2 * pi) * P,                                        # phase → days
         coi_ok    = df$time >= EF & df$time <= nrow(df) - EF)                  # trustworthy (outside COI) mask
}

# ---- settled pre/post summary with block SEs ------------------------------------------------
# One estimate per P-long (annual) cycle in a COI-buffered window, then value ± dispersion/√n_blocks.
block_stat <- function(t, v, circ = FALSE) {
  b  <- (t - min(t)) %/% P
  bv <- as.numeric(tapply(seq_along(t), b, function(i) if (circ) CMEAN(v[i]) else mean(v[i])))
  n  <- length(bv)
  if (circ) {
    R    <- Mod(mean(exp(1i * bv)))                       # resultant length of block phases (1 = locked, 0 = scattered)
    disp <- if (R < 1) sqrt(-2 * log(R)) else 0           # circular sd
  } else disp <- sd(bv)
  list(value = if (circ) CMEAN(bv) else mean(bv),
       se    = if (n > 1) disp / sqrt(n) else NA_real_, n = n)
}

# Settled windows come from the `phase` column: buffer EF from BOTH the series ends and the
# disturbance. Real phases are pre / disturbance / post (no synthetic "transition"); the
# disturbance may be a single day or a short span (fernow_WS-5), so we buffer from its full extent.
wave_summary <- function(tr) {
  ds <- min(tr$time[tr$phase == "disturbance"])          # first disturbance step
  de <- max(tr$time[tr$phase == "disturbance"])          # last disturbance step (span for WS-5)
  N  <- max(tr$time)
  wins <- list(pre  = tr$time >= EF      & tr$time <= ds - EF,     # ≥ EF from series start AND from disturbance onset
               post = tr$time >= de + EF & tr$time <= N  - EF)     # ≥ EF from disturbance end AND from series end
  one <- function(sel) {
    t  <- tr$time[sel]
    co <- block_stat(t, tr$coherence[sel]); xp <- block_stat(t, tr$xpower[sel])
    ar <- block_stat(t, tr$amp_ratio[sel]); an <- block_stat(t, tr$angle[sel], circ = TRUE)
    tibble(n_blocks  = co$n,                                            # cycles the SEs rest on
           coherence = co$value, coherence_se = co$se,
           xpower    = xp$value, xpower_se    = xp$se,
           amp_ratio = ar$value, amp_ratio_se = ar$se,
           phi       = an$value,                 phi_se = an$se,          # phase difference φ, radians (circular SE)
           lag       = an$value / (2*pi) * P, lag_se = an$se / (2*pi) * P)  # same φ in day units
  }
  bind_rows(pre = one(wins$pre), post = one(wins$post), .id = "phase") %>%
    mutate(phase = factor(phase, c("pre", "post")))
}

# ---- model-based residual (whole series, log space) -----------------------------------------
# Predict log(response) from a pre-fit predictor: predicted = gain · climate(t − lag). lag & gain
# are fit on `pre` only (cross-correlation peak + no-intercept OLS), so the residual sits at ~0
# through pre and departs where coupling changes.
fit_pre <- function(df) {
  pre <- df[df$phase == "pre", ]; cl <- pre$climate; rs <- pre$log_response; n <- length(rs)
  Ls  <- 0:round(0.6 * P)                                              # physically plausible lags only (< ~0.6 season)
  cr  <- vapply(Ls, function(L) cor(rs[(L + 1):n], cl[seq_len(n - L)]), numeric(1))  # cor(log(response)(t), climate(t−L))
  L   <- Ls[which.max(cr)]
  x   <- cl[seq_len(n - L)]; y <- rs[(L + 1):n]
  list(lag = L, gain = sum(x * y) / sum(x * x))                        # OLS slope, no intercept
}

resid_trace <- function(df) {
  fp <- fit_pre(df); L <- fp$lag; N <- nrow(df)
  pred <- rep(NA_real_, N); if (L < N) pred[(L + 1):N] <- fp$gain * df$climate[seq_len(N - L)]
  resid <- df$log_response - pred
  out <- tibble(time = df$time, phase = df$phase,
                resid  = resid,
                dresid = c(NA, diff(smooth_ma(resid, 61))))            # smooth THEN diff so d/dt isn't pure noise
  attr(out, "lag") <- L; attr(out, "gain") <- fp$gain; out
}

# ---- shared plotting furniture (band shading matches analysis III) --------------------------
phase_fill <- c(pre = "#9ecae1", disturbance = "grey45", post = "#fdae6b")
bar_fill   <- phase_fill[c("pre", "post")]
phase_bands <- function(ph) ph %>% filter(phase != "disturbance") %>%
  group_by(phase) %>% summarise(xmin = min(time), xmax = max(time), .groups = "drop")

phase_band_layers <- function(d) {
  bands <- phase_bands(d); td <- d$time[d$phase == "disturbance"]
  list(geom_rect(data = bands, aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf, fill = phase),
                 alpha = 0.30, inherit.aes = FALSE),
       geom_vline(xintercept = td, linetype = 2, colour = "grey70"),
       scale_fill_manual(values = phase_fill, guide = "none"))
}

# whole-series residual + its smoothed derivative on a secondary axis, phases shaded AND labelled.
plot_resid <- function(df, title) {
  rt <- resid_trace(df); L <- attr(rt, "lag"); g <- attr(rt, "gain")
  bands <- phase_bands(df)
  ytop <- max(abs(rt$resid), na.rm = TRUE)
  sc   <- ytop / max(abs(rt$dresid), na.rm = TRUE)
  ggplot() +
    phase_band_layers(df) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_line(data = rt, aes(time, dresid * sc), colour = "#d94801", linewidth = 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_line(data = rt, aes(time, resid),       colour = "#238b45", linewidth = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_text(data = mutate(bands, x = (xmin + xmax) / 2),
              aes(x = x, y = ytop, label = phase), vjust = 1, size = 3, colour = "grey25") +
    scale_y_continuous(sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / sc, name = "d(residual)/dt  (smoothed, orange)")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time (days)", y = "residual = log(response) − predicted  (green)",
         subtitle = sprintf("pre-fit predictor:  log(response)(t) ≈ %.3f · climate(t − %d)", g, L))
}

# time-resolved band metrics; COI-contaminated edges blanked; phases shaded.
metric_labs <- c(coherence = "coherence", xpower = "cross power", amp_ratio = "amp ratio", lag = "app. lag (d)")
plot_band_trace <- function(tr, title) {
  m <- names(metric_labs)
  long <- tr %>%
    mutate(across(all_of(m), ~ ifelse(coi_ok, ., NA_real_))) %>%
    select(time, all_of(m)) %>%
    pivot_longer(-time, names_to = "metric") %>%
    mutate(metric = factor(metric, m))
  ggplot() +
    phase_band_layers(tr) +
    geom_line(data = long, aes(time, value), linewidth = 0.3, na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1, strip.position = "left",
               labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
    labs(title = title, x = "time (days)", y = NULL)
}

# reshape a summary tibble to long (id keys × metric × value × se).
metrics_long <- function(sm, keys) {
  m <- names(metric_labs)
  val <- sm %>% select(all_of(c(keys, m))) %>%
    pivot_longer(all_of(m), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")
  se  <- sm %>% select(all_of(c(keys, paste0(m, "_se")))) %>%
    rename_with(~ sub("_se$", "", .x), all_of(paste0(m, "_se"))) %>%
    pivot_longer(all_of(m), names_to = "metric", values_to = "se")
  left_join(val, se, by = c(keys, "metric")) %>% mutate(metric = factor(metric, m))
}

# pre/post bars per metric, ±1 SE (block SE) so effect size is read against uncertainty.
plot_wave_bars <- function(sm, title) {
  dat <- metrics_long(sm, "phase")
  ggplot(dat, aes(phase, value, fill = phase)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = value - se, ymax = value + se), width = 0.25, colour = "black", na.rm = TRUE) +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", nrow = 1, labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = bar_fill, guide = "none") +
    labs(title = title, x = NULL, y = NULL)
}

# the settled pre/post table — same columns every site.
emit_table <- function(sm, caption)
  cat(kable(sm %>% select(phase, n_blocks, coherence, xpower, amp_ratio, phi, lag),
            digits = 2, caption = caption), sep = "\n")
# response (discharge) analyzed in log space → add log_response once at load (prep floored zeros).
sites <- c("hbef_w1", "hjandrews_GSWS10", "fernow_WS-3", "fernow_WS-5", "santa_barbara_MC06")
D <- lapply(sites, function(s)
  read_csv(sprintf("%s__series_daily.csv", s), show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(phase = factor(phase, c("pre", "disturbance", "post")),
           log_response = log(response)))
names(D) <- sites
# section labels come from the tracked CSVs themselves (disturbance/class carried by the prep) —
# no dependency on any external file, so 04 builds from the committed *__series_daily.csv alone.
label <- setNames(vapply(D, function(d)
  sprintf("%s (%s, %s)", d$site[1], d$disturbance[1], d$class[1]), character(1)), sites)
# One coherency transform per site → reused by every image, trace and table below (~1 min total).
WC <- lapply(D, wco)
TR <- Map(band_trace, WC, D)
SM <- lapply(TR, wave_summary)

Data provenance (ms_interp)

prov <- bind_rows(lapply(D, function(d) tibble(
  site = d$site[1],
  climate_interp_pct  = round(100 * mean(d$climate_interp), 1),
  response_interp_pct = round(100 * mean(d$response_interp), 1))))
cat(kable(prov, caption = "Percent of each channel that is MacroSheds gap-fill (modelled), per site. MC06's precip driver is ~88% modelled — read its coherence accordingly."), sep = "\n")
Percent of each channel that is MacroSheds gap-fill (modelled), per site. MC06’s precip driver is ~88% modelled — read its coherence accordingly.
site climate_interp_pct response_interp_pct
hbef_w1 0.0 0
hjandrews_GSWS10 13.4 0
fernow_WS-3 0.0 0
fernow_WS-5 0.0 0
santa_barbara_MC06 87.6 0

hbef_w1 — chemical addition (pulse)

Chemistry manipulation, not water yield — the seasonal precip→discharge coupling should be a near-null across the disturbance. A good reference for what the wavelet returns when nothing hydrologic happened.

plot_resid(D[["hbef_w1"]], paste(label["hbef_w1"], "— model residual & its derivative"))

Figure 1. hbef_w1 — model residual (green) + smoothed derivative (orange, right axis); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — wavelet coherence")

Figure 2. hbef_w1 — wavelet coherence spectrum (time × period). Arrows = phase; pale COI parabola = edge-unreliable. Note how normalized coherence saturates near 1 across most of the annual band. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — synchrony at the P≈365 band, through time")

Figure 3. hbef_w1 — band (P≈365) metrics through time; COI edges blanked, phases shaded. 

emit_table(SM[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = annual cycles the SEs rest on)")
hbef_w1 — band synchrony, settled pre vs post (n_blocks = annual cycles the SEs rest on)
phase n_blocks coherence xpower amp_ratio phi lag
pre 14 0.96 0.03 4.74 2.98 173.26
post 21 0.97 0.04 3.21 2.65 154.12 
plot_wave_bars(SM[["hbef_w1"]], "hbef_w1 — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 4. hbef_w1 — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE (block SE).

hjandrews_GSWS10 — debris flow (pulse)

A physical channel disturbance that could alter the storm→flow routing. Watch the band amplitude ratio and apparent lag across 1986. (~13% of precip is modelled infill.)

plot_resid(D[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], paste(label["hjandrews_GSWS10"], "— model residual & its derivative"))

Figure 5. hjandrews_GSWS10 — model residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — wavelet coherence")

Figure 6. hjandrews_GSWS10 — wavelet coherence spectrum. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — synchrony at the P≈365 band, through time")

Figure 7. hjandrews_GSWS10 — band metrics through time.

plot_wave_bars(SM[["hjandrews_GSWS10"]], "hjandrews_GSWS10 — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 8. hjandrews_GSWS10 — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE.

fernow_WS-3 — chemical addition (chronic)

Chronic acidification, open-ended — “post” is a persistently manipulated regime. Chemistry target, so like hbef expect little hydrologic signature, but the long record gives the wavelet many annual cycles to average.

plot_resid(D[["fernow_WS-3"]], paste(label["fernow_WS-3"], "— model residual & its derivative"))

Figure 9. fernow_WS-3 — model residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["fernow_WS-3"]], "fernow_WS-3 — wavelet coherence")

Figure 10. fernow_WS-3 — wavelet coherence spectrum. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["fernow_WS-3"]], "fernow_WS-3 — synchrony at the P≈365 band, through time")

Figure 11. fernow_WS-3 — band metrics through time. 

emit_table(SM[["fernow_WS-3"]], "fernow_WS-3 — band synchrony, settled pre vs post")

post 29 0.99 0.08 2.25 -1.32 -76.90 
plot_wave_bars(SM[["fernow_WS-3"]], "fernow_WS-3 — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 12. fernow_WS-3 — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE.

fernow_WS-5 — timber harvest (pulse)

The clearest water-yield disturbance (2007 clearcut): reduced transpiration raises discharge and can sharpen the storm response — the site where the seasonal-band amplitude ratio has the most reason to move.

plot_resid(D[["fernow_WS-5"]], paste(label["fernow_WS-5"], "— model residual & its derivative"))

Figure 13. fernow_WS-5 — model residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["fernow_WS-5"]], "fernow_WS-5 — wavelet coherence")

Figure 14. fernow_WS-5 — wavelet coherence spectrum. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["fernow_WS-5"]], "fernow_WS-5 — synchrony at the P≈365 band, through time")

Figure 15. fernow_WS-5 — band metrics through time.

plot_wave_bars(SM[["fernow_WS-5"]], "fernow_WS-5 — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 16. fernow_WS-5 — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE.

santa_barbara_MC06 — burn (pulse)

Semi-arid, intermittent flow, and a ~88% modelled precip driver — the hardest case. A fire can sharply increase runoff, but here both the intermittency (log-floor) and the infilled driver undercut the assumptions; read the wavelet output as a demonstration of the method’s limits, not a measurement of the burn.

plot_resid(D[["santa_barbara_MC06"]], paste(label["santa_barbara_MC06"], "— model residual & its derivative"))

Figure 17. santa_barbara_MC06 — model residual (green) + derivative (orange); phases shaded, dashed = disturbance. 

plot_wc(WC[["santa_barbara_MC06"]], "santa_barbara_MC06 — wavelet coherence")

Figure 18. santa_barbara_MC06 — wavelet coherence spectrum. Read against the ~88% modelled precip driver. 

plot_band_trace(TR[["santa_barbara_MC06"]], "santa_barbara_MC06 — synchrony at the P≈365 band, through time")

Figure 19. santa_barbara_MC06 — band metrics through time. 

emit_table(SM[["santa_barbara_MC06"]], "santa_barbara_MC06 — band synchrony, settled pre vs post")

plot_wave_bars(SM[["santa_barbara_MC06"]], "santa_barbara_MC06 — band metrics, pre vs post (±1 SE)")

Figure 20. santa_barbara_MC06 — pre vs post band metrics, ±1 SE.

Cross-site summary

Post-minus-pre change in the four band metrics per site, each Δ carrying its propagated SE (√(se_pre² + se_post²)). In analysis II the synthetic scenarios separated cleanly and taught the lesson that coherence alone is not enough; here the question is which of these real sites, if any, moves a power/amplitude channel beyond its (now well-resolved) error.

sm_all <- bind_rows(SM, .id = "site")
delta <- metrics_long(sm_all, c("site", "phase")) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = phase, values_from = c(value, se)) %>%
  mutate(delta    = value_post - value_pre,
         delta_se = sqrt(se_pre^2 + se_post^2),
         site     = factor(site, sites))
cat(kable(delta %>% transmute(site, metric = metric_labs[as.character(metric)],
                              pre = value_pre, post = value_post, delta, delta_se),
          digits = 2, caption = "pre → post change in band metrics, all sites (delta_se = propagated ±1 SE)"), sep = "\n")

ggplot(delta, aes(site, delta, fill = site)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = delta - delta_se, ymax = delta + delta_se), width = 0.25, colour = "black", na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(metric_labs)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", guide = "none") +
  labs(title = "Post − pre change by band metric (free y axes; ±1 SE)", x = NULL, y = "post − pre") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1))

Figure 21. Post − pre change per site, faceted by band metric (free y), ±1 propagated SE. The lesson from analysis II carries over: coherence barely moves anywhere (it saturates on real data too), so if anything registers it is in cross-power / amplitude.

Takeaways — the native wavelet view on real synchrony, next to analysis III. The lesson analysis II taught on synthetic data reappears unprompted on real watersheds: WaveletComp’s normalized, smoothed coherence saturates near 1 across the whole record at every site (see the red spectra), so — exactly as in the synthetic Desync case — coherence is not a usable discriminator here; whatever real change exists must be read from un-normalized cross-power and band amplitude, which the wavelet still resolves with well-populated error bars (decades of cycles, unlike the synthetic ~2). Against analysis III: the peak/moments lens frayed on the episodic driver (an unstable “period,” a degenerate MC06), while the wavelet keeps a clean, time-resolved amplitude/lag read but hands back a falsely reassuring coherence — two methods, two different partial views, each incomplete in its own way. That, not any single site’s number, is the point of running both: the choice of synchrony metric decides what you can and cannot see, and on real, less-deterministic coupling neither view is sufficient alone. ```
